page: Microsoft Azure D32as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv5 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The instance features 32 dedicated vCPUs derived from 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled, providing two threads per core. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache.

This server type does not include local storage, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external persistent storage volumes. There are no GPUs bundled with this instance, and it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, utilizing AMD's Secure Virtual Machine technology.

Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests scoring in the top 25 percent at 2702.76. Multi-core performance falls in the middle 50 percent average range, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 43243.6. Memory performance is notable, with a strong PassMark memory score in the top 25 percent, alongside average memory bandwidth speeds of 74.32 GB/sec for large block reads and 44.85 GB/sec for large block writes. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench image processing also rank in the top 25 percent, while LLM inference tasks show average performance for small and medium models, but drop to the bottom 25 percent for prompt processing on large 70B models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D32as_v5 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of local NVMe storage. The absence of built-in local storage means storage costs are decoupled from compute resources. This configuration is appropriate for workloads such as web serving, application servers, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv5 family, powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities and memory scores, both ranking in the top 25 percent, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. Large-model LLM inference represents a performance tradeoff, falling into the bottom 25 percent. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, delivering strong single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and Premium Storage support, offering balanced compute and memory.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-capacity memory for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM with strong memory benchmark scores
Premium Storage capable with no local storage bundled
Strong single-core CPU and multi-threaded decompression performance
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
